office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: Incumbent Julio Cortes faces disability and labor advocate Annie Fitzgerald and independent Thomas Kelly, whose platform evidence is sparse.

candidates.summary: Fitzgerald is a caregiver, investigator, and disability advocate supporting Medicare for All, labor rights, progressive revenue, and affordable housing.
highlights: Supports Medicare for All and progressive revenue [S2]
Supports labor, disability rights, and affordable housing [S2]

experience.basis: She brings advocacy and professional experience but no documented elected record.

healthcare.basis: She explicitly supports Medicare for All.

candidates.summary: Cortes is an incumbent representative focused on progressive revenue, homelessness services, labor protections, behavioral health, and infrastructure.
highlights: Supports progressive revenue and public infrastructure [S2]
Supports homelessness services and behavioral health [S2]

spending.basis: His priorities emphasize services and public infrastructure.

safety.basis: His approach emphasizes homelessness services and behavioral health.

experience.basis: He is an incumbent representative with prior nonprofit and city experience.

candidates.summary: Kelly is an independent candidate whose official records establish his candidacy but no policy or professional profile.
